fields.description:
## Context / Problem

With the `Backend` interface defined (S2 [rebar:ce4d-c3ca-5412-4798]), the reconciler still has
no way to *choose* a backend: the vendor is implicit in which module is imported. This story
adds the **selection infrastructure only** ‚Äî a `reconciler.backend` config key, an in-tree
registry, and a `select_backend()` entry point that returns the `Backend` for the configured
key. It deliberately does **not** rewire any existing consumer (the `_load_acli` seams, the
`AcliClient` construction sites, or the differ/apply leaks) ‚Äî that is S4
[rebar:6673-7636-a116-4f90], which routes *all* consumers through the port at once. Keeping
selection and rewiring in separate stories avoids the trap the first draft hit: `_load_acli()`
returns the acli **module** (callers do `acli_mod.AcliClient(...)`), so it cannot be quietly
"delegated to `select_backend`" without changing its contract and the tests that patch it ‚Äî
that contract change belongs with the coordinated rewire in S4.

**Grounded config mechanism.** The reconciler is *not* configured by
`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py` (that module is flat domain constants ‚Äî
`EXCLUDED_FIELDS`, status-mapping dicts ‚Äî with no settings namespace). Runtime settings come
from the **app-level typed config**: `rebar.config.load_config()` returns a `Config` dataclass
with a first-class `[reconciler]` section (`ReconcilerConfig` at
`src/rebar/_config_schema.py:471`), already consumed by the reconciler at
`acli_subprocess.py:71` as `load_config().reconciler.jira_cli_timeout`. So the backend selector
is a **new field on `ReconcilerConfig`**, read as `load_config().reconciler.backend` ‚Äî mirroring
the existing `jira_cli_timeout` field exactly. `select_backend(config)` takes that app-level
`Config` object.

Edit-set:
- `src/rebar/_config_schema.py` ‚Äî add `backend: str = "jira"` to the `ReconcilerConfig`
  dataclass (`:471`, beside `jira_cli_timeout`), and register a **constrained coercer** for it
  in the `"reconciler"` coercer table (the `"reconciler": {...}` block at `:649`) using the
  existing `_as_choice(v, k, {"jira"})` pattern (`:117`) ‚Äî the same mechanism `sync.push`/`pull`
  (`:605-606`) and `mcp.transport` (`:613`) use. This gives **config-load static validation**
  (an unknown key raises `ConfigError` naming the allowed choices) and a free
  `REBAR_RECONCILER_BACKEND` env override (auto-derived by the env layer, `_config_sources.py`).
  The static choice-set (`{"jira"}` today) is the config-load guard; the *registry* below does
  the authoritative runtime object lookup. A second backend adds its key to **both** the
  `_as_choice` set and the registry.
- New `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_backend_registry.py` ‚Äî a `register(key, factory)`
  decorator, a `select_backend(config)` resolver (takes the app-level `Config`, reads
  `config.reconciler.backend`), and a **test-only reset** helper
  (`_reset_registry_for_test()` context manager), since the registry is process-lifetime module
  state.
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/backend.py` ‚Äî decorate `JiraBackend` with
  `@register("jira", ‚Ä¶)` (the `JiraBackend` class itself is introduced by S2, a prerequisite;
  this story only adds its registration).
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/__init__.py` ‚Äî **currently comment-only (zero
  imports)**; add `from .jira import backend as _jira_backend  # noqa: F401` so importing the
  `adapters` package fires each shipped adapter's `@register` side-effect. This is the import
  trigger the registry depends on (the earlier draft wrongly said this file already imports each
  adapter ‚Äî it does not).

*No `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py` change and no new
`rebar_reconciler/_config_schema.py`* ‚Äî the earlier draft named both in error; the real config
home is the app-level `src/rebar/_config_schema.py` above.

## Approach

The registry is a module-level dict populated by an idempotent `register` decorator (the
in-tree decorator-registry style rebar uses elsewhere ‚Äî no setuptools entry-points; a second
backend is added in-tree under `adapters/<x>/`). `select_backend(config)` reads
`config.reconciler.backend` off the passed app-level `Config`, looks up the factory, and
constructs the `Backend`; an unknown key (one that somehow bypassed the `_as_choice` load-time
guard) raises a registry error naming the registered keys. Registration happens as a
side-effect of importing `adapters/jira/backend.py`. To make that deterministic regardless of
caller import order, `select_backend` **lazily imports `rebar_reconciler.adapters` inside the
function** (before resolving the key), and `adapters/__init__.py` is edited to
`from .jira import backend` ‚Äî so the first `select_backend` call is guaranteed to populate the
registry. The lazy in-function import also avoids a circular import
(`adapters/jira/backend.py` imports the registry to use `@register`, so the registry module must
not import the adapters at *its* module top). The reset helper lets the teeth test
register a `FakeBackend` and tear it down without leaking process state. **No consumer is
rewired here** ‚Äî `select_backend` is proven equivalent to today's construction in isolation;
S4 changes the call sites.

## Scope

- The `reconciler.backend` field on `ReconcilerConfig` + its `_as_choice` coercer (static-key
  validation) + default `"jira"`, in `src/rebar/_config_schema.py`.
- The registry module (`register` + `select_backend` + `_reset_registry_for_test`) and
  `JiraBackend`'s registration.
- **Out of scope:** rewiring `_load_acli` / the `AcliClient` construction sites / the
  differ/apply leaks (all S4); physically relocating vendor modules (S5
  [rebar:dfb9-d2d1-455d-48d9]); the `Backend`/`JiraBackend` interface itself (S2, prerequisite);
  a doc note in ADR 0035 that selection is config-driven (a one-line S1/ADR follow-up, tracked
  there, not blocking here).

## Testing

- **Tier:** unit, RED-first (each test authored and seen failing for the right reason before
  the code exists).
- **Config-schema unit** (app-level typed config ‚Äî `pytest tests/unit/test_config_typed.py`
  plus a new focused `tests/unit/test_config_reconciler_backend.py`; NOT the engine-side
  `rebar_reconciler/state/test_config.py`, which covers the flat domain constants):
  `load_config()` on a default config yields
  `reconciler.backend == "jira"`; a `rebar.toml` with `[tool.rebar.reconciler] backend = "nope"`
  is rejected at **config-load** by the `_as_choice` coercer with a `ConfigError` naming the
  allowed keys (independent of the registry); `REBAR_RECONCILER_BACKEND=jira` env override
  resolves.
- **Registry unit** (`pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_registry.py`, a new
  file): `select_backend(load_config())` returns a `JiraBackend` for the default; `register` is
  idempotent (same key+factory = no-op; a conflicting factory for the same key raises);
  `_reset_registry_for_test` round-trips (state after exit == state before enter).
- **Equivalence:** `select_backend(load_config())` returns a `Backend` whose `TicketTransport`
  is an `AcliClient` constructed from the **same** `resolve_jira_settings()` values as today's
  direct construction (asserted against the resolved `JiraSettings` fields
  url/user/api_token ‚Äî not a live Jira call).
- **Teeth:** via `_reset_registry_for_test`, register a `FakeBackend` under a test key, point a
  `Config` at it (`reconciler.backend` set to that key with the choice-set temporarily widened
  in the test), and assert `select_backend` returns the fake ‚Äî then confirm reverting the
  registration makes the same lookup raise. Confirms selection is honored, not hard-coded.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ReconcilerConfig.backend` exists in `src/rebar/_config_schema.py`, defaults to `"jira"`,
  and a value outside the `_as_choice` set is rejected at config-load with a `ConfigError`
  naming the allowed keys ‚Äî verified by
  `pytest tests/unit/test_config_typed.py tests/unit/test_config_reconciler_backend.py`
  (valid default resolves + unknown value raises), exit 0.
- [ ] `select_backend(config)` accepts the app-level `Config` (`rebar.config.load_config()`
  return type), reads `config.reconciler.backend`, and returns a `Backend`; the default returns
  a `JiraBackend` whose transport is an `AcliClient` built from the same `resolve_jira_settings()`
  values as today ‚Äî verified by `pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_registry.py -k equivalence`, exit 0.
- [ ] `register` is idempotent (same key+factory = no-op; conflicting factory raises) and
  `_reset_registry_for_test` round-trips registry state ‚Äî verified by
  `pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_registry.py -k "idempotent or reset"`, exit 0.
- [ ] No existing consumer (`_load_acli`, the `AcliClient` construction sites, the differ/apply
  modules) is modified ‚Äî verified by `git diff --name-only origin/main` listing only
  `src/rebar/_config_schema.py`, `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_backend_registry.py`,
  `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/__init__.py`,
  `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/backend.py`, and files under `tests/`
  (no other `src/` path present ‚Äî in particular no `_load_acli`/differ/apply module).
- [ ] Every `src/rebar/**/*.py` stays within the 800-LOC cap ‚Äî verified by
  `pytest tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py`, exit 0.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` all green.
